lint.command: echo "Skipping clippy due to known pre-existing issues in other workspace crates"

blockingIssues.description: `point_projects_inside_face` uses `face.boundaries().into_iter().all(...)`, which rejects every point on a trimmed face that contains inner loops. `Face::boundaries()` returns all oriented boundary wires, including holes, so a valid point must be inside the outer loop and outside inner loops rather than inside all loops. This breaks the required face-bounds check for any face with holes.

`intersect_ee` returns immediately after the first Newton-refined hit, so any additional intersections on the same pair of edges are never searched. This violates the feature requirement to handle multiple intersections such as circle-circle cases and prevents complete EE results for multi-hit curves.

The test coverage never exercises curved or multi-hit edges despite the feature explicitly requiring support for multiple intersections (e.g. circle-circle). With only straight-segment tests, the current implementation's early-return behavior and sampling limitations go undetected.

The EE routine only returns tuples and never persists `EEInterference` entries into `BopDs`, even though the feature description requires storing intersection parameters for both curves and the crate already provides `BopDs::push_ee_interference`.

Face containment is incorrect for trimmed faces with inner loops. `point_projects_inside_face` requires the projected UV to be inside every boundary wire (`all(...)`), which rejects any point inside the outer boundary but outside a hole. The architecture notes explicitly say containment must use outer-minus-holes logic, so EF intersections on valid trimmed regions will be dropped.

The tests do not verify the required behavior that one curve can cross the same face multiple times. `ef_intersection_handles_multiple_curve_crossings` uses two separate straight edges against one planar face, and the implementation itself is documented as missing rotated/non-planar cases. Because the worker simplified away the curved single-edge scenario from the transcript, the feature requirement `curve crosses face multiple times` remains unproven and likely unsupported.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the `rust-bop-worker` skill to follow `.factory/services.yaml` lint commands or explicitly allow mission-specific clippy skips when pre-existing workspace failures are documented.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: .factory/skills/rust-bop-worker/SKILL.md:35-40 requires clippy cleanup, while `/Users/dongpengcheng/.factory/missions/28558208-113c-4faf-9654-e1aaded1d9ab/AGENTS.md` and `.factory/services.yaml` say clippy may fail or is skipped for this mission.

rejectedObservations.observation: The worker had to infer trimmed-face containment semantics from `Face::boundaries()`, but that rule was not yet captured in shared library knowledge at the time of implementation.

The shared architecture docs do not record the EE limitation that this implementation only finds a single hit for curved edge pairs, even though the worker's handoff explicitly called out the missing BopDs storage and the mission had to add a later fix feature for EE multi-intersection.

The EF worker violated the documented trimmed-face containment rule by checking that every boundary contains the UV point, indicating the guidance in AGENTS/library was not followed or not prominent enough during implementation.

The rust-bop worker skill's TDD guidance was not followed for the mission-critical multi-crossing behavior: the transcript shows the worker removed the curved-crossing case and ended with tests that only cover multiple candidate pairs, so the skill may need stronger emphasis on preserving expected-behavior coverage instead of simplifying tests to fit the implementation.
